label.purpose: Persistent field labels and tool names.

motion.name: state
motion.purpose: Hover, focus, selection, and validation state changes.

motion.name: panel
motion.purpose: Responsive editor panel transitions without choreography.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Commits the next meaningful creator action.

components.name: Labeled Field
components.description: Collects planner and customer details with persistent labels.

components.name: Planner Block
components.description: A movable, validated content area inside the planner canvas.

narrative.overview: Planner Studio is a professional work surface with the calm, material clarity of a well-run creative studio. The interface stays restrained so customer content and printable page previews remain visually dominant.
keyCharacteristics: Restrained application chrome around a true-to-size planner preview.
Precise 14px layout rhythm carried from the page system into the editor.
Professional controls with creative color used for orientation and selection.
Flat, print-aware surfaces with strong borders and no decorative effects.
Immediate, accessible feedback for every edit and validation result.

rules.name: The Orientation Rule
rules.body: Color groups and orients; it never fills space merely to make the interface feel creative.

rules.name: The Two-Family Rule
rules.body: Georgia belongs to planner artifacts; Aptos belongs to the product interface.

rules.name: The Flat Workshop Rule
rules.body: If a panel needs a wide soft shadow to feel separate, its hierarchy or boundary is wrong.

dos: Do keep the planner preview visually dominant and the application chrome restrained.
Do use 14px as the core layout rhythm and preserve exact printable safe areas.
Do pair every color-coded state with text, shape, or an icon.
donts: Don't create generic AI-generator styling, interchangeable dashboard decoration, or unexplained automatic choices.
Don't let template-first experiences erase the creator's judgment.
Don't allow drag interactions to create overlap, off-page content, invalid gutters, or inaccessible pointer-only workflows.
